audit_items.proof_trace: damon_free_target() (core.c:335-342) frees regions and kfrees the target struct but never calls put_pid(t->pid); the sysfs layer's damon_sysfs_before_terminate callback (sysfs.c:2308), which correctly calls put_pid(), is registered only after damon_sysfs_apply_inputs() succeeds (sysfs.c:2449). When damon_sysfs_set_targets() succeeds (vaddr targets added via find_get_pid()) but damon_sysfs_set_schemes() returns -ENOMEM, damon_sysfs_build_ctx() calls damon_destroy_ctx() → damon_destroy_targets() which leaks every struct pid reference held by those targets.
audit_items.attack_scenario: A privileged user configures DAMON via sysfs with one or more vaddr/fvaddr monitoring targets (valid PIDs) plus enough schemes to exhaust memory during damon_sysfs_mk_scheme(); the resulting ENOMEM error triggers damon_destroy_ctx() on the half-built context, leaking one struct pid reference per added target — repeated invocations can progressively exhaust struct pid objects or prevent PID reuse.
